---
description: "Use when writing any player-facing text — room names, room descriptions, item descriptions, command feedback, error messages, or help text. Defines the game's narrative voice."
applyTo: ["src/data/**", "src/engine/**"]
---
# Tone of Voice

> **Status: TBA** — The game theme has not been defined yet. Fill in this file once the theme is chosen. The placeholders below show what to document.

## Theme

_Not yet defined. Example: "Victorian gothic horror", "whimsical fairy-tale", "hard-boiled noir"._

## Voice

_Describe the narrator's personality. Example: "Dry, sardonic, second-person present tense. Short sentences. No exclamation marks."_

## Vocabulary

_List preferred and forbidden words or phrases. Example:_

| Prefer | Avoid |
|--------|-------|
| _TBA_ | _TBA_ |

## Room descriptions

_Guidelines for writing `Room.description`. Example: "Two sentences maximum. Start with a sensory detail. End with the most important interactive element."_

## Item descriptions

_Guidelines for writing `Item.description`. Example: "One sentence. Focus on what makes the item unusual or useful."_

## Command feedback

_Guidelines for parser responses. Example: "Terse. Never blame the player. Use 'You can't do that.' not 'Invalid command.'"_

## Examples

_Once the theme is defined, add 2–3 annotated sample room/item descriptions here as a style reference._
